notes: SHUFFLE WEEK PUSH. Push press first = 50/side x8! The reorder worked — W4 couldn't even get 50 overhead, now 8/8/7. Fresh shoulders make a huge difference. Lateral raise found 20s and they're doable. Right shoulder/lower blade had some tension (slept weird?) — affected a few exercises. Incline DB at 55 was gassed from push press — got 10/8/8/10. Pec deck jumped to 115x12 clean for all 3 sets. Chest press machine was last and completely died — started at 170 and couldn't even do 3, worked down to 140x6. Being last instead of first dropped it by 35lbs. Straight arm pulldown at 110 is the real working weight now.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: FIRST EXERCISE — fresh shoulders. 50/side x8 x2, then died on 7th rep set 3. W4: couldn't even get 50 overhead. The shuffle proved it was fatigue, not strength. 50/side is the real working weight. Hard but decent form.
Found 20s! Doable for 15. Right side had tension in lower shoulder blade — possibly slept weird. Affected form a bit but completed all sets. W4: 15s were too easy. 20 is the right weight.
Chest press was taken so did this before pec deck. Bench position at notch 5 = about 45 degrees. Gassed from push press — inconsistent reps (10/8/8/10). Last set pushed through for 10. Still at 55.
Chest press still taken. 110x12 easy, jumped to 115 — clean for both sets! W4: 115x9 was failure. Now 115x12 clean. Big jump. 115 is the working weight, ready for 120 next time.

LAST exercise — completely gassed from push press + incline + pec deck. Started at 170 and immediately died. Worked down to 140x6. W4 (first exercise): 175x8. Being last dropped working weight by 35lbs. This is the cost of the shuffle — but push press at 50 was worth it.
55x12 all 3 sets. Right side weaker from shoulder tension but not painful. W4: 50x12. Up 5lbs. Right side struggles to hold statically even though it should be stronger.
First set felt easy, then it destroys you fast. 12/12/10(9). Same pattern as W4. 65 is solidly the working weight. That first-set-easy-then-death feeling is the tricep isolation catching up.
110 is the real working weight now — W4's 105x12 was too easy but 110x12 is genuinely hard. Died at 9 on set 3. Huge progression from W3's spontaneous 90x10.

notes: KB Explore day. Followed the workout as written. Shoulders dead from yesterday's push press breakthrough (50/side x8 is a lot of overhead). Dropped C&P to 6 reps instead of 8 — smart call with the shoulder twinge still lingering. Flow and skill work went well.

exercises.notes: Followed the workout. Flow is getting smoother week over week.
Skill work block. Shoulders tired but manageable at 25.
Solid.
Light and easy.
Dropped from x8 to x6 — shoulders dead from yesterday's push press. Right call. Still hard at 50.
Finished up.

notes: Rough pull day — depleting super early. Pull-ups fragmented: 9/9/6+2/4+2+2 = 34 total but felt way worse than the number suggests. Right shoulder clicky on warmup cat-cow but felt fine during work. DB rows easy — 75x10 PR! Preacher curl early in session (shuffle): 15/side x10 was the real number, 20 and 17.5 both failed fast. Lat pulldown at 160 was weirdly easy but also tired — wider grip than pull-ups (near the bend). Seated cable row died at 130x10 on set 3. Bicep cable curls depleted at 42.5x10. Just a low-energy day overall.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

exercises.notes: 34 total but super fragmented. Depleting early — felt good after short rests but couldn't sustain. Right shoulder felt the pull-ups but not painfully. W4: 33 (also rough). Something about this week — maybe cumulative fatigue from push press breakthrough.
75x10 PR! Form broke on last 2 both sides but completed all 10. Easy compared to pull-ups — confused why some days rows are easy and pull-ups are hard. No grip problems.

SHUFFLE TEST: preacher curl early (after pull-ups only, before cable curls). 15/side x10 is the honest working weight — hard but full ROM. 20 was too much (only 2), 17.5 only got 4. Drop set 15→10 to failure. W3/W4 preacher was always last and completely dead. Now we know: 15/side x10 is the real number.

160 is a jump from W4's 150. Weirdly easy first set but also tired and lazy. Shoulder felt it slightly. Grip wider than pull-ups — holding near the bend. Different grip width = different exercise emphasis (wider = more lat isolation, less bicep).
Same pattern every week. 50x20 first set fine, degrades from there. Consistent.
130x12/12/10. Third set died. Consistent with W4. 130 is the working weight.
Hard from set 1 today. Form breaking earlier than usual. Low energy day.

Only got 10s instead of 12s. Biceps already worked from preacher curl earlier. The shuffle trade-off: preacher gets honest numbers, cable curls get fewer reps. 42.5x10 is probably the right weight now that preacher comes first.

notes: Saturday legs (shifted from Friday for furniture moving). Squats at 70/side x8 for all 3 working sets — felt safe and strong, way better than early 65s where form felt unsafe. Thinks leg press swap earlier in the program helped build confidence. RDL bumped to 70/side on last set — fresh legs are a thing. Leg press at 5 plates, ROM limited by mobility not strength — tried 6 plates x8 at the end and it was hard but doable. Calves FINALLY burned at 6 plates x20 — first time ever feeling these. Leg curl consistent at 110x12. Walking lunges completed all sets, still hard but no nausea like W1. Big improvement across the board.
cardio: 12 min zone 2 run to gym + 12 min zone 2 run back

sets: 70/side x8 (really good range, felt safe and strong)
exercises.notes: 70x8 across all 3 working sets. First set cautious/not full ROM, improved each set. Felt safe and strong — huge confidence gain vs early weeks at 65 where it felt unsafe. Thinks leg press work has helped squat confidence. W4: 70x8 was a PR on the last set only. Now it's the baseline for all sets.
65 felt good/great for 2 sets. Bumped to 70 on set 3 — felt great. Fresh legs = easy progression. W3: 65 was the working weight. 70 is ready to be the new baseline.

sets: 5 plates/side x12 (beat ROM on last set)
exercises.notes: 5 plates x12 for working sets. ROM limited by mobility, not strength — should go deeper. Started feeling strong toward end, actually beat ROM with no fatigue on last set. Tried 6 plates x8 after — hard but doable. 6 plates is the next target after a warmup round. Using lower mount point (longer ROM, harder).

FIRST TIME calves and legs actually burned from calf raises! 5 plates easy, bumped to 6 and did 4 sets of 20. Last set finally felt the burn. Progression: W3 5 plates easy → W4 6 plates easy → W5 6 plates x4 sets = finally burning. Maybe 7 plates next time, or stay at 6 with more sets.
110x12 consistent across 3 sets. Third set form broke on last 3 reps — not full ROM. 110 is the right weight. Try 115 on set 3 next time if first 2 sets are clean.

sets: completed all sets (ugly last set with rest)
exercises.notes: All full sets completed. Last set ugly with lots of rest breaks. Still very hard but didn't want to throw up like W1 — that's improvement. Walking lunges remain the finisher that finishes you.
